key_insight: The study shows that combining clinical language models with traditional text features can improve independent TNM stage prediction from pathology reports.
clinical_relevance: Accurate TNM staging extraction from pathology text could support faster cancer data abstraction and more consistent oncology analytics, but it is not ready for clinical deployment.
method: Framed TNM staging as three multi-label classification tasks: T, N, and M.
Compared TF-IDF with embeddings from ClinicalBERT, BioBERT, and PubMedBERT.
Used Logistic Regression, LightGBM, FFNN, and Wide Residual Networks for prediction.
evidence_signal: LightGBM with TF-IDF performed best in training (AUROC up to 0.9524 for N), while test performance was mixed and dropped across evaluation sets.
watch_item: Generalizability remains limited due to class imbalance and the difficulty of modeling long, complex clinical documents.
paper_title: CaresAI at SMM4H-HeaRD 2026: Predicting TNM Staging
